Re: Contributions to Officehol,der Accounts
Dear John:
Recently, I asked several questions regarding the
application of the rrtransfer banrr at Government Code section
85304. My inquiry was prompted by the Cornmj-ssionrs recent Advice
Letter to Frederick K. Lowell (r-91--064, 2/6/91) in which the
Comrnission advised, in part:
rrMoreover, section 85304 continues to
prohibit candidates for state or l-ocal- office
fron transferring campaign funds to any
candidate who is subject to any valid
contribution lirnits, including those l-imits
established by focal j urisdi-cti-ons . 'l
I advised you that we represent San Francisco Mayor Art
Agnos, San Francisco does have a valid contribution limit
ordinance of $500 per election. 
However, the city Attorney has
opined that a committee maintained after a successful election
etfort and controlled by a San Francisco officehol-der, which is
not for re-election purposes, is not subject to the contribution
Iimitation ordinance. These so-ca}Ied t'of f iceholderrr or
rrFriendsrr accounts, whife not subject to san Francisco limits,
rernain subject to the Political Reform Act (PRA) ' 
Mayor Agnos
has two "uididut.-"ontrof led campaign committees: Friends of Art
aq.o=, 7.D. #747235 which is hj-s ofiiceholder account from his
i6az iru.p.ign, and Agnos For Mayor, r'D' #903014 which is his
rggr ."-L:-"6tion acc5unt. The tirst 
committee is not subject to
i."-nii""i=co 
contribution lirnitations, the second is'
Based upon the Commissionts advice, referenced above' the
Aqnos for Mayor cornmj-ii"t nut returned all contributions received
?;;;".il;t"c'uii-to."i" 
state or rocar candidates and has advised
ln"="-.."aidates of -it" Co^roi=tionrs interpretation that such
contributions are not perrnitted '


Q:
A:
1.
John w. Wal1ace, Esq.
July 3, L99L
Page 2
In our telephone conversation, f asked several additional
questions regarding the types of carnpaign contributions which
coufd be received from other 'rcandidatesrt for the UgygE account
and the Friends account. Your responses, as I understood them,
are as follo$/s 3
May a candidate for state or loca1 office nake a
personal contribution or a contribution fron his or her
control,l,ed committee to Friends?
Yes. There is no rrtransfer banrr provision for oersonal
contributions, and committee contributions are also
unlimited as long as the Friends account is not used as
a conduit to the llayor account since that account is
under a valid loca1 linit.
May a candidate for federal office (not presently a
California state or local public official) 
or a non-
California candidate make a contribution to Mavor or
Friends frorn campaign funds?
Yes. The transfer ban at Government Code section 85304
e/ould not apply to such candidates or their comrnittees.
can a state candidate, lrho has been or who is
concurrently a federal candidate, make a contribution
to Mayor or Friends from that candidate's federal
campaign account?
An unlimited contribution can be made to the Friends
account, but a contribution to the UgygI account woul,d
violate the transfer ban since the transfer ban
prohibits any state or local candidate from
transferring funds (even from a federal committee) to
any other candidate where there is a valid contribution
I i.rnit .
we would appreciate your written confirmation of our
telephone conversation as described above. If you have any
questions, or need any additional information, please do not
hesitate to contact ne.
